description: Create a two-column content slide on CRO priorities and resilience, with two narrative text blocks and two survey respondent quote cards, plus a right sidebar of illustrative images.

header.kicker: SECOND ANNUAL EY/EIU GLOBAL INSURANCE RISK MANAGEMENT SURVEY

left.title: 2. The many different threats to resilience are threaded through the CRO agenda.

items.text: Cyber remains the number one risk priority for CROs, cited by 66% of this year's respondents, up from 53% last year. That increase can be attributed, to some extent, to increasing geopolitical risk, which presents the threat of more intense cyber attacks in the eyes of many CROs.

The engagement of the EY organization and IIF with risk leaders across the industry confirms that operational resilience is a focal point because of its close links to cyber, technology, third-party and regulatory risk.
Increased operational resilience can be seen as a target outcome – a key goal in setting strategies and tactics to manage many other types of risks.
CROs are acting on multiple fronts to enhance resilience, including governance, critical business service frameworks, and tech/disaster recovery.
Some firms are beginning to think about their recession playbooks to help ensure financial resilience in the event of an economic downturn.

Survey respondent quote: Risk management has become a vital partner not only in measuring impacts but also in enhancing the company's resilience through understanding and evaluating potential future risks.

right.title: 3. Investments in talent and disruptive technology are top priorities, but they must be aligned and synchronized to optimize returns.

items.text: CROs report increased investments in multiple types of technologies, including emerging technologies and foundational tools.
The goal is to achieve tech maturity, pay off tech debt and build strong IT infrastructures to support next-generation risk management capabilities.
Investments in key skills and talent, such as AI and data science, are necessary to drive the most value from tech investments, putting a premium on holistic strategies.
Investments in technology should be aligned to broader technology transformation efforts across the business.
CROs should engage with business leaders to identify additional risk insights that may be delivered by deployments of new tech for internal, back-office, and customer-facing usage.
Survey respondent quote: There is a lot more focus on people who can use the technology. The combination of human capital and the technology is a big driver of the value.

sidebar.title: Illustrative imagery
sidebar.body: Right sidebar contains three decorative images supporting the themes of cyber, people and technology.
